personas.name: Budget-Conscious Mindfulness Seeker
personas.description: A user who wants high-quality meditation support without subscriptions, ads, or aggressive monetization.
goals: Avoid ads, paywalls, and forced upgrades
Use an app that feels trustworthy and community-oriented
pain_points: Other meditation apps lock key features behind subscriptions
Donation prompts can become annoying if they interrupt the experience
Users do not want monetization pressure during meditation
context: Comparing Medito with paid wellness apps
Using meditation regularly but on a limited budget
Willing to donate voluntarily if the experience stays respectful
constraints: Core content must remain accessible without payment
Donation requests should be optional and non-intrusive

personas.name: First-Time Meditator
personas.description: A beginner who is starting meditation for the first time and needs clear guidance, simple navigation, and approachable lessons.
goals: Find sessions quickly without feeling overwhelmed
pain_points: Many meditation apps feel confusing or too advanced
Large content libraries can be hard to navigate at first
New users need reassurance that they are using the app correctly
context: Using short guided sessions to learn technique
Exploring the app from a phone during daily routines
constraints: The interface must be simple for new users
Beginner content must be easy to discover

personas.name: User Seeking Calm, Sleep, and Emotional Relief
personas.description: A user who relies on meditation content to reduce stress, manage anxiety, sleep better, and feel emotionally stable.
goals: Use meditation during emotionally difficult moments
pain_points: Stress, insomnia, panic, or low mood can make it hard to focus
Users may need different content types for sleep versus anxiety relief
Poor playback or missing content can disrupt a calming routine
context: Using the app at night, during stressful moments, or during mental health challenges
Returning to specific sessions that help with sleep or grounding
constraints: Relevant content must be easy to locate
Playback must remain uninterrupted during listening

personas.name: Consistency-Driven Daily User
personas.description: A repeat user who meditates often and wants streaks, reminders, and progress features to support long-term habit formation.
goals: Recover quickly when progress data looks wrong
pain_points: Users want habit support without being punished unfairly
Progress tracking can feel unreliable if sessions are not recorded correctly
context: Opening the app every day or almost every day
Using challenges, streaks, and daily sessions as motivation
constraints: Habit features must reflect completed sessions consistently

personas.name: Mobile User Facing Playback and Usability Friction
personas.description: A user who values Medito but is blocked by audio interruptions, sync problems, missing offline access, or feature gaps.
goals: Use helpful controls such as reminders, timers, and offline playback
pain_points: Audio can stop when the screen turns off or when earbuds are connected
Cross-device streaks and progress can fail
Some useful controls are missing or hard to find
context: Using the app on different phones, with earbuds, or in low-connectivity settings
Trying to meditate while sleeping, traveling, or switching devices
constraints: Playback must stay stable across common device states
Important controls should be visible and easy to use
